name: HPING3

core_modules.name: PACKET_CRAFTING
core_modules.title: Custom Packet Assembly Engine
core_modules.description: Enables manual configuration of TCP/UDP/ICMP headers, window sizes, flags, and data payloads.

core_modules.name: FLOOD_ENGINE
core_modules.title: High-Velocity Traffic Flooding
core_modules.description: Generates high volume of packets (SYN flood, UDP flood) to test rate limiters, firewalls, and network resilience.

attackPaths.desc: Though not applicable here (expires 2028), domains nearing expiration could be monitored for hijacking opportunities.

keyTakeaways.title: DNS_SPOOFING_VULNERABILIT
keyTakeaways.content: Without DNSSEC, the domain remains theoretically vulnerable to DNS cache poisoning and spoofing attacks.

attackPaths.title: DNS_SPOOFING_VULNERABILIT
attackPaths.desc: Without DNSSEC, the domain remains theoretically vulnerable to DNS cache poisoning and spoofing attacks.

attackPaths.title: MAN-IN-THE-MIDDLE_POTENTI
attackPaths.desc: Attackers could potentially intercept and modify DNS responses to redirect users to malicious sites.

attackPaths.title: DNS_CACHE_POISONING
attackPaths.desc: A red team could potentially demonstrate DNS poisoning attacks against recursive resolvers serving Google.com.

attackPaths.title: BGP_HIJACKING_+_DNS_ATTAC
attackPaths.desc: Combine route hijacking with DNS spoofing to redirect traffic from legitimate Google services.

scenarios.objective: Test if any loopholes exist in CAA implementation
objectiveList: Test if any loopholes exist in CAA implementation
Google's choices likely reflect calculated decisions balancing security, performance and compatibility
High-traffic sites often make different security trade-offs than smaller sites

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_FINDING
keyTakeaways.content: Consider certificate type recommendations based on client analysis

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_FINDING
keyTakeaways.content: For large organizations like Google, TLS configuration decisions often involve complex trade-offs between security, global accessibility, performance, and backward compatibility. Their configurations may intentionally deviate from standard recommendations to support specific business requirements or legacy clients. A red team assessment should recognize these nuances while still highlighting potential security improvements.

keyTakeaways.title: FIREWALL_BYPASS
keyTakeaways.content: Custom packet flag crafting helps bypass stateless firewall configurations by emulating established sessions.

keyTakeaways.title: ICMP_PROBING
keyTakeaways.content: Triggering specific TCP replies is useful for network discovery when standard ICMP queries are filtered.

keyTakeaways.content: Simulating high-volume SYN request streams helps validate WAF and rate limiter security policies.

keyTakeaways.title: SECURITY_FINDING
keyTakeaways.content: When conducting red team assessments against similar high-traffic sites, it's important to recognize that these compatibility decisions aren't oversights but strategic choices to serve business requirements. Security recommendations should acknowledge these constraints while presenting risk-based improvement options.

troubleshooting.problem: hping3 requires raw socket access (Permission Denied)
troubleshooting.cause: Low-level packet assembly requires root privileges.

troubleshooting.problem: Packets dropped or filtered by firewalls
troubleshooting.cause: Network filters block custom TCP flag probes.
troubleshooting.resolution: Configure fragmentation or source port spoofing.

security: Ensure all execution requests with HPING3 are performed within authorized scope. Log all output artifacts securely and protect the integrity of local credential wordlists.
